acceptanceCriteria: TI-12 (mutation-score decline) moves from rejected to conditional shadow, restricted to repositories that already run a pinned mutation tool as a configured check with comparable scores over the same configuration; no default mutation run is introduced and the falsifiable-tests table gains a must-fire/must-not-fire pair consistent with that scope.

TI-13 and TI-15 move from advisory only to shadow candidate (#22) at explicitly lower priority than TI-05/TI-06/TI-09/TI-07/TI-08, with their confidence cells split into a deterministic configured-rule-match component and an advisory weakening-interpretation component.
TI-11's confidence cell distinguishes measurement accuracy (given identical tool, version, configuration, and scope) from the advisory interpretation that a decline indicates weakening.

TI-01's false-positive-risk wording claims only detection of a configured-check definition change without inferring intent, and its must-not-fire case becomes an unrelated .rigor/policy.json edit that leaves the checks array untouched.
TI-08 gains a must-not-fire case for snapshot-only changes without accompanying implementation changes.
The catalog states explicitly that the implemented TI-02/TI-03/TI-04 gate constrains cross-attempt progress classification (reduced/incomparable), not verification, review, or merge outcomes.

The shadow-candidate summary section and scenario tables remain consistent with the updated enforcement levels, and every existing label (deterministic fact / tool-derived measurement / advisory interpretation) is unchanged.
CHANGELOG.md documents the amendment under an Unreleased heading, attributing the revisions to a recorded Codex consultation without describing Codex agreement as approval or verification.
npm run test:all, npm run bench, and claude plugin validate . --strict pass, and git diff --check is clean.
note: Policy permits transmission; minimize content and re-check destination controls.
